attrs.alt: PIM for Groups: one group activation grants multiple Azure RBAC and Entra roles simultaneously

content.text: What PIM for Groups Solves

PIM for Groups (generally available as of 2024) enables PIM-managed membership in Entra ID security groups. Because a group can simultaneously hold Azure RBAC roles, Entra directory roles, and enterprise app memberships, a single group activation can grant a user a coordinated set of permissions across multiple systems. This is a significant improvement over managing N separate eligible assignments for N roles.

Bundled privilege: a 'production operator' role might require Contributor on prod-rg (Azure RBAC), User Administrator (Entra role), and access to a monitoring app (app role). Without PIM for Groups, each requires a separate eligible assignment and separate activation. With PIM for Groups, one eligible group membership covers all three.

Consistent expiry: all permissions granted via the group expire at the same time — when the group membership expires. There is no risk of one role expiring before another when they were granted for the same task.

Simplified assignment management: adding a user to one PIM group grants them eligibility for the full bundled permission set. Removing the group membership revokes all associated access simultaneously.
Reduced assignment count: each user holds one eligible group membership rather than N separate eligible role assignments, saving Azure RBAC assignment quota.

PIM for Groups is the recommended approach when a job function requires access to both Azure resources and Entra directory objects simultaneously. The single-activation experience is significantly less friction than activating multiple separate roles.
Requirements and Constraints
Requirement
Detail
License
Microsoft Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance
Group type
Entra ID security group with isAssignableToRole = true (set at creation, immutable)
Nested groups
PIM-enabled groups cannot be nested inside other PIM-enabled groups
Group scope
Must be a cloud-only group (not synced from on-premises AD)
Owner requirement
The group must have at least one permanent owner (cannot be owner-less)
API
Microsoft Graph identity governance APIs (not Azure Resource Manager)
Creating a PIM-Enabled Group and Assigning Roles

A group must be created with isAssignableToRole = true before PIM can manage its membership. This flag is immutable — you cannot enable it on an existing group. The group then receives role assignments (Azure RBAC, Entra roles, app roles) and becomes the proxy for all of those permissions.

# Enable PIM for an existing Entra security group
# Prerequisite: group must be a 'role-assignable group' (isAssignableToRole = true)
# Role-assignable groups are created once and cannot be converted after creation.
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Group.ReadWrite.All','PrivilegedAccess.ReadWrite.AzureADGroup'

# Create a role-assignable group (must be done at group creation)
$group = New-MgGroup -DisplayName 'prod-ops-team' `
    -MailEnabled $false `
    -SecurityEnabled $true `
    -MailNickname 'prod-ops-team' `
    -IsAssignableToRole $true   # This is the key flag

# Enable PIM management for this group
# Once enabled, memberships can be eligible (PIM) or active
$pimGroup = New-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupEligibilityScheduleRequest `
    -AccessId 'member' `
    -PrincipalId $group.Id `
    -GroupId $group.Id
# Note: 'activating' PIM for a group is done by creating the first
# eligible schedule request — the group becomes PIM-managed automatically.

# Assign Azure RBAC role to the group
New-AzRoleAssignment `
    -ObjectId          $group.Id `
    -RoleDefinitionName 'Contributor' `
    -Scope             '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/prod-rg'

# Assign Entra role to the group
$userAdminRoleId = (Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleDefinition -Filter "displayName eq 'User Administrator'").Id
New-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleAssignment `
    -RoleDefinitionId $userAdminRoleId `
    -PrincipalId $group.Id `
    -DirectoryScopeId '/'
Adding Eligible Members
Once the group is role-assigned, you add users as eligible members via the PIM Groups API. The eligible membership does not grant any access until the user activates it.

# Add a user as an eligible member of a PIM-managed group
# The user can activate group membership; activation grants ALL roles the group holds.
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PrivilegedAccess.ReadWrite.AzureADGroup'

$groupId = (Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'prod-ops-team'").Id
$userId  = (Get-MgUser -UserId 'alice@contoso.com').Id

# Create eligible member assignment
$eligibleRequest = @{
    action           = 'adminAssign'
    justification    = 'Production ops team access — annual access grant'
    accessId         = 'member'   # 'member' or 'owner'
    principalId      = $userId
    groupId          = $groupId
    scheduleInfo     = @{
        startDateTime = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o')
        expiration    = @{ type = 'afterDuration'; duration = 'P180D' }
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

New-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupEligibilityScheduleRequest `
    -BodyParameter ($eligibleRequest | ConvertFrom-Json)

# Alice can now activate group membership in the PIM portal under
# PIM → My roles → Groups → Eligible assignments
# Activating grants temporary Contributor + User Administrator simultaneously.
You can add users, service principals, and other groups as eligible members — subject to the nesting constraint.
The accessId field is either 'member' (normal group membership, which inherits all role assignments) or 'owner' (can manage the group and its settings).
Eligible membership assignments support expiry (set in scheduleInfo.expiration). After expiry, the user can no longer activate — the eligibility must be renewed.
Like standard PIM, you can configure approval requirements and MFA for group membership activation in the PIM portal under PIM → Groups → Settings.
User-Initiated Activation
Users activate their eligible group membership in the PIM portal under 'My roles → Groups' or via the Microsoft Graph API. Activation creates a temporary active membership that expires automatically.

# User self-activates their group membership via MS Graph
# (normally done via PIM portal; scriptable for automation)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PrivilegedAccess.ReadWrite.AzureADGroup'

$groupId = (Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'prod-ops-team'").Id
$myId    = (Get-MgUser -UserId (Get-MgContext).Account).Id

# Find my eligible schedule instance
$eligible = Get-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupEligibilityScheduleInstance `
    -Filter "groupId eq '$groupId' and principalId eq '$myId'"

# Submit activation request
$activateRequest = @{
    action                              = 'selfActivate'
    justification                       = 'Deploying blue/green switchover INC-5001'
    accessId                            = 'member'
    principalId                         = $myId
    groupId                             = $groupId
    linkedEligibleScheduleId            = $eligible.Id
    scheduleInfo = @{
        startDateTime = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o')
        expiration    = @{ type = 'afterDuration'; duration = 'PT4H' }  # 4 hours active
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

New-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupAssignmentScheduleRequest `
    -BodyParameter ($activateRequest | ConvertFrom-Json)

# After activation, Alice is a temporary active member of prod-ops-team.
# This makes her Contributor on prod-rg AND User Administrator in Entra ID.
# Both expire automatically after 4 hours.
After activation, the user is a temporary group member. Any Azure RBAC roles or Entra roles assigned to the group now apply to the user until the activation expires.
The user must re-authenticate (obtain a new token) for the new group membership to appear in their access token. In the Azure portal, signing out and back in is sufficient.

Group membership activation events appear in both the Entra audit log (Add member to group — PIM activation) and as derivative events in the Azure Activity Log for any RBAC operations performed during the session.
Listing Eligible and Active Members
Standard group-listing APIs (Get-MgGroupMember) do not distinguish between permanent and PIM-activated temporary members. Use the privileged access schedule APIs for an accurate view.

# List all PIM group eligible and active assignments
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PrivilegedAccess.Read.AzureADGroup'

$groupId = (Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'prod-ops-team'").Id

# Eligible members (those who can activate)
Write-Host '=== Eligible Members ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Get-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupEligibilityScheduleInstance `
    -Filter "groupId eq '$groupId'" |
    Select-Object @{n='Principal';e={(Get-MgUser -UserId $_.PrincipalId).DisplayName}},
                  AccessId, StartDateTime, EndDateTime

# Currently active members (those who activated their eligibility)
Write-Host '=== Currently Active Members ===' -ForegroundColor Green
Get-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupAssignmentScheduleInstance `
    -Filter "groupId eq '$groupId' and assignmentType eq 'Activated'" |
    Select-Object @{n='Principal';e={(Get-MgUser -UserId $_.PrincipalId).DisplayName}},
                  AccessId, StartDateTime, EndDateTime

# Note: standard Get-MgGroupMember will show BOTH permanent and active-PIM members
# but cannot distinguish between them. Use the schedule APIs above for accurate state.
PIM for Group Owners

PIM for Groups also manages the 'owner' access level separately from 'member'. An eligible owner can activate owner permissions to manage the group's membership and settings, without being a permanent group owner.

# PIM for group OWNER role (controls who can modify the group itself)
# 'owner' access in a PIM group lets the user add/remove members,
# change group properties, and manage the PIM settings for the group.
# This is a separate access level from 'member'.

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PrivilegedAccess.ReadWrite.AzureADGroup'

$groupId = (Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'prod-ops-team'").Id
$adminId = (Get-MgUser -UserId 'groupadmin@contoso.com').Id

# Make someone an eligible OWNER (can manage group when activated)
$ownerRequest = @{
    action        = 'adminAssign'
    justification = 'Group admin rotation'
    accessId      = 'owner'   # <-- owner, not member
    principalId   = $adminId
    groupId       = $groupId
    scheduleInfo  = @{
        startDateTime = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o')
        expiration    = @{ type = 'afterDuration'; duration = 'P365D' }
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

New-MgIdentityGovernancePrivilegedAccessGroupEligibilityScheduleRequest `
    -BodyParameter ($ownerRequest | ConvertFrom-Json)

# Best practice: keep permanent owners to a minimum (1-2 break-glass).
# Use eligible owner assignments for operational group management.

Use eligible owner assignments for operational group management (adding members, changing settings). Keep permanent owners to a minimum — ideally just one or two break-glass accounts that can recover the group if PIM is unavailable.
Nested Group Limitations and Design Patterns

PIM for Groups has a key limitation: PIM-enabled groups cannot be nested within other PIM-enabled groups. This means group hierarchies must be designed around flat, purpose-specific PIM groups rather than nested organizational structures.

# Limitation: nested group membership is NOT supported in PIM for Groups.
# A PIM-enabled group CANNOT be a member of another PIM-enabled group.
# If you try to add a PIM group as a member of another PIM group, the operation fails.

# Limitation: non-role-assignable groups cannot be PIM-enabled.
# Groups with isAssignableToRole = false cannot have PIM-managed memberships.
# This cannot be changed after group creation — you must create a new group.

# Limitation: PIM for Groups requires Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance.

# Design pattern: use flat, purpose-specific PIM groups
# Rather than: prod-ops-team (PIM) contains devs-subgroup (regular group)
# Do:          prod-ops-team (PIM) with direct user eligible memberships
# And:         bundle all needed permissions onto the one PIM group

# Check if a group is role-assignable:
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Group.Read.All'
(Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'prod-ops-team'").IsAssignableToRole
# Returns True if PIM-eligibility can be configured, False otherwise.
Design Pattern
When to Use
Trade-off
One PIM group per job function
Standard approach — prod-ops-team, dev-team, security-ops
More groups to manage; cleaner activation UX
Permanent outer group + PIM inner groups
Organizational groups (all-devs) contain permanent members; PIM groups for specific access
Outer group cannot itself be PIM-enabled
Per-resource PIM groups
prod-storage-contributors, prod-kv-officers (one per resource)
Fine-grained control; more activation steps for cross-resource work
Bundled privilege groups
prod-full-ops includes all roles needed for incident response
Higher blast radius per activation; fewer steps for common workflows
Infrastructure as Code

Terraform's azuread provider supports creating role-assignable groups and assigning roles to them. PIM eligible membership assignments require Microsoft Graph API calls since the provider doesn't yet include that resource type.
Auditing PIM Group Activations

PIM group activations appear in the Entra audit log as 'Add member to group (PIM activation)' events. These carry the user identity, group name, and justification text, providing a complete trail of when each bundle of permissions was in use.

# Audit PIM group activations
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'

$start = (Get-Date).AddDays(-7).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')

# Get PIM group activation events
Get-MgAuditLogDirectoryAudit `
    -Filter "activityDisplayName eq 'Add member to group (PIM activation)' and activityDateTime ge $start" |
    Select-Object ActivityDateTime,
        @{n='User';e={$_.InitiatedBy.User.UserPrincipalName}},
        @{n='Group';e={($_.TargetResources | Where-Object Type -eq 'Group').DisplayName}},
        @{n='Justification';e={
            ($_.AdditionalDetails | Where-Object Key -eq 'justification').Value
        }},
        Result
Group deactivation (expiry or manual) appears as 'Remove member from group (PIM deactivation)'.
For Azure RBAC access: the group's role assignment always exists; the effective access is controlled by whether the user is an active group member at the time of the ARM request.
Combine audit of group activations with audit of ARM operations during the same time window to build a complete picture of what changes were made during each elevated session.
Choosing Between PIM Groups and Individual PIM Assignments
Scenario
Prefer PIM Group
Prefer Individual Assignment
User needs 3+ roles simultaneously for a job function
Yes — one activation grants all
No — 3 separate activations is friction
Single role at a specific scope
No — group overhead not worthwhile
Yes — direct eligible assignment is simpler
Team of 10 users needing the same access bundle
Yes — add all 10 as eligible group members
No — 10×N assignments is unwieldy
Access requires different expiry times per role
No — all roles expire with the group membership
Yes — set individual expirations per role
Automation pipeline needing Azure RBAC only
No — managed identity direct assignment is simpler
Yes — no group overhead
Cross-system access (RBAC + Entra + App roles)
Yes — single activation for all systems
No — multiple separate activations required
Summary
PIM for Groups enables PIM-managed membership in role-assignable Entra ID security groups. One eligible membership covers all roles assigned to the group — Azure RBAC, Entra roles, and app roles.
Groups must be created with isAssignableToRole = true. This flag is immutable — plan your group structure before creation.
PIM group activation uses MS Graph APIs (not ARM). Eligible and active memberships are tracked via the privileged access schedule instance APIs.
Both 'member' and 'owner' access levels support eligible assignments. Eligible owners can activate group management permissions without being permanent group owners.
PIM groups cannot be nested within other PIM groups. Design flat, purpose-specific groups rather than hierarchies.
Audit group activations via the Entra audit log 'Add member to group (PIM activation)' events, which include the user identity, group name, and justification.
